name: tact

metadata.name: tact
metadata.summary: Taxonomic addition for complete trees: Adds tips to a backbone phylogeny using taxonomy simulated with birth-death models

documentation:
# TACT: Taxonomic Addition for Complete Trees

[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/tact.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/tact/)
[![Build status](https://github.com/jonchang/tact/workflows/Python%20package/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jonchang/tact/actions)
[![Docker Hub](https://img.shields.io/docker/pulls/jonchang/tact.svg)](https://hub.docker.com/r/jonchang/tact)

TACT is a Python app for stochastic polytomy resolution. It uses birth-death-sampling estimators across an ultrametric phylogeny to generate branching times for unsampled taxa, using taxonomic information to compatibly place new taxa onto a backbone phylogeny.

## Getting started with TACT

* [Installing](https://tact.jonathanchang.org/install/)
* [Introduction and background](https://tact.jonathanchang.org/background/)
* [Example analysis](https://tact.jonathanchang.org/tutorial/)
* [Troubleshooting problems](https://tact.jonathanchang.org/troubleshooting/)

## Citation

TACT is described more fully in its manuscript. If you use TACT, please cite:

* Chang, J., Rabosky, D. L., & Alfaro, M. E. (2019). Estimating diversification rates on incompletely-sampled phylogenies: theoretical concerns and practical solutions. Systematic Biology. doi:[10.1093/sysbio/syz081](https://doi.org/10.1093/sysbio/syz081)

TACT owes its existence to much foundational work in the area of stochastic polytomy resolution, namely PASTIS and CorSiM.

* Thomas, G. H., Hartmann, K., Jetz, W., Joy, J. B., Mimoto, A., & Mooers, A. O. (2013). PASTIS: an R package to facilitate phylogenetic assembly with soft taxonomic inferences. Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 4(11), 1011–1017. doi:[10.1111/2041-210x.12117](https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210X.12117)

* Cusimano, N., Stadler, T., & Renner, S. S. (2012). A New Method for Handling Missing Species in Diversification Analysis Applicable to Randomly or Nonrandomly Sampled Phylogenies. Systematic Biology, 61(5), 785–792. doi:[10.1093/sysbio/sys031](https://doi.org/10.1093/sysbio/sys031)

## Sponsorship

Please consider sponsoring the ongoing maintenance of TACT via [GitHub Sponsors](https://github.com/sponsors/jonchang).

Initial development was supported by a National Science Foundation Doctoral Dissertation Improvement Grant (DEB-1601830).

modules.name: tact.fastmrca
documentation: Singleton object that helps speed up MRCA lookups.

functions.name: tact.fastmrca.initialize
documentation: Initialize the fastmrca singleton with a tree.

functions.name: tact.fastmrca.bitmask
documentation: Gets a bitmask for the taxa in `labels`, potentially in parallel.

functions.name: tact.fastmrca.get
documentation: Pulls a MRCA node out for the taxa in `labels`.

functions.name: tact.fastmrca.fastmrca_getter
documentation: Helper function for submitting stuff.

modules.name: tact.validation
documentation: Various validation functions for `click` classes and parameters.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: tact.validation.validate_outgroups
documentation: Validates an `outgroups` parameter, by splitting on commas and transforming underscores to spaces.

functions.name: tact.validation.validate_newick
documentation: Validates a Newick tree, using appropriate defaults.

functions.name: tact.validation.validate_tree_node_depths
documentation: Validates a DendroPy tree, ensuring that the node depth is equal for all tips.

functions.name: tact.validation.validate_taxonomy_tree
documentation: Validates a taxonomy tree.

classes.name: tact.validation.BackboneCommand
documentation:
Helper class to validate a Click Command that contains a backbone tree.

At a minimum, the Command must contain a `backbone` parameter, which is validated by `validate_newick`
and checked to ensure it is a binary tree.

If the command also contains a `taxonomy` parameter, representing a taxonomic phylogeny,
this is also validated to ensure that the DendroPy TaxonNamespace is non-strict superset
of the taxa contained in `backbone`. An optional `outgroups` parameter may add
other taxa not in the `taxonomy`.

If the command also contains an `ultrametricity_precision` parameter, the
ultrametricity of the `backbone` is also checked.

modules.name: tact.generic_monophyly
documentation: Module that provides a generic monophyly singleton data object.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: tact.exceptions.TactError
documentation: Base class for errors raised by TACT.

classes.name: tact.exceptions.DisjointConstraintError
documentation: Exception raised when a set of constraints lead to a disjoint implied age interval.

functions.name: tact.cli_taxonomy.fix_file
documentation:
Slurps a file, and does various checks and fixes:
* Sorts the file
* Ensures column names are unique

functions.name: tact.cli_taxonomy.build_taxonomic_tree
documentation:
Builds a taxonomic tree given a filename. Last column is assumed to
be a species name. All ranks must nest completely within the next
highest rank.

functions.name: tact.cli_taxonomy.main
documentation:
Generates a taxonomic tree from TAXONOMY.

TAXONOMY is a comma-separated values file with several requirements.

 
* Each row represents a single species.
* Each column represents a taxonomic rank.
* The columns must be arranged from most inclusive to least inclusive,
  with the last column as the species name.
  - e.g. Family,Genus,Species
* Each rank must be named
* Each rank must be unique
  - OK: Cichlidae,Cichla,Cichla temensis
  - NO: Cichlidae,Cichlidae,Cichla temensis
  - NO: Cichlidae,,Cichla temensis

This script makes **many** assumptions about its input for speed. Check
the example taxonomy in the examples/ folder for guidance.

modules.name: tact.cli_check_trees
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: tact.cli_check_trees.main
documentation:
Check a SIMULATED phylogeny for consistency with its backbone source tree and a taxonomy.

The SIMULATED phylogeny should have been generated by the tact_add_taxa script.
All phylogenies should be in Newick format.

modules.name: tact.tree_util
documentation: Functions specifically to handle DendroPy tree objects.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: tact.tree_util.get_birth_death_rates
documentation:
Estimates the birth and death rates for the subtree descending from
`node` with sampling fraction `sampfrac`. Optionally restrict to a
Yule pure-birth model.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.get_monophyletic_node
documentation: Returns the node or None that is the MRCA of the `species` in `tree`.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.get_ages
documentation:
Returns the list of ages of the children of a given `node`,
optionally including the `node`'s age if `include_root` is True.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.get_tip_labels
documentation: Returns a `set` of tip labels for a node or tree.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.edge_iter
documentation:
Iterates over the child edge of `node` and all its descendants.
Can optionally be filtered by `filter_fn`.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.get_tree
documentation: Gets a DendroPy tree from a path and precalculate its node ages and bipartition bitmask.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.update_tree_view
documentation:
Mutates a DendroPy tree object with updated node ages and bipartition bitmask. We also
correct for minor ultrametricity errors.

Returns a list of tip labels.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.is_binary
documentation: Is the subtree under `node` a fully bifurcating tree?

functions.name: tact.tree_util.is_ultrametric
documentation:
Is the `tree` ultrametric, within a specified `tolerance`?

Uses the relative difference between minimum and maximum root-to-tip distances.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.get_short_branches
documentation: Yields an iterator of especially short edges under `node`.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.compute_node_depths
documentation: Returns a dictionary of node depths for each node with a label.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.graft_node
documentation:
Grafts a node `graft` randomly in the subtree below node
`graft_recipient`. The attribute `graft.age` must be set so
we know where is the best place to graft the node. The node
`graft` can optionally have child nodes, in this case the
`edge.length` attribute should be set on all child nodes if
the tree is to remain ultrametric.

We graft things "below" a node by picking one of the children
of that node and forcing it to be sister to the grafted node
and adjusting the edge lengths accordingly. Therefore, the node
*above* which the graft lives (i.e., the one that will be the child
of the new graft) must fulfill the following requirements:

1. Must not be the crown node (cannot graft things above crown node)
2. Must be younger than the graft node (no negative branches)
3. Seed node must be older than graft node (no negative branches)
4. Must not be locked (intruding on monophyly)

functions.name: tact.tree_util.lock_clade
documentation: Locks a clade descending from `node` so future grafts will avoid locked edges.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.unlock_clade
documentation: Unlocks a clade descending from `node` so new tips can be grafted to its edges.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.count_locked
documentation: How many edges under `node` are locked?

functions.name: tact.tree_util.is_fully_locked
documentation: Are all the edges below `node` locked?

functions.name: tact.tree_util.get_min_age
documentation:
Gets the minimum possible age that could be generated in a clade under `node`,
assuming that grafts to locked edges are restricted.

functions.name: tact.tree_util.get_age_intervals
documentation:
Gets the (possibly disjoint) interval that could be generated in the
clade under `node`, assuming that grafts to locked edges are restricted.

modules.name: tact.lib
documentation: Functions to handle various numerical operations, including optimization.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: tact.lib.get_bd
documentation:
Converts turnover and relative extinction to birth and death rates.

Args:
    r (float): turnover or net diversification (birth - death)
    a (float): relative extinction (death / birth)

Returns:
    (float, float): birth, death

functions.name: tact.lib.get_ra
documentation:
Converts birth and death to turnover and relative extinction rates.

Args:
    b (float): birth rate
    d (float): extinction rate

Returns:
    (float, float): turnover, relative extinction

functions.name: tact.lib.wrapped_lik_constant
documentation:
Wrapper for birth-death likelihood to make optimizing more convenient.

Args:
    x (float, float): turnover, relative extinction
    sampling (float): sampling fraction (0, 1]
    ages (list): vector of node ages

Returns:
    (float): a likelihood

functions.name: tact.lib.wrapped_lik_constant_yule
documentation:
Wrapper for Yule likelihood to make optimizing more convenient.

Args:
    x (float): birth rate
    sampling (float): sampling fraction (0, 1]
    ages (list): vector of node ages

Returns:
    (float): a likelihood

functions.name: tact.lib.two_step_optim
documentation:
Conduct a two-step function optimization, first by using the fast L-BFGS-B method,
and if that fails, use simulated annealing.

Args:
    func (callable): function to optimize
    x0 (tuple): initial conditions
    bounds (tuple): boundary conditions
    args (list): additional arguments to pass to `func`

Returns:
    params (tuple): optimized parameter values

functions.name: tact.lib.optim_bd
documentation:
Optimizes birth and death parameters given a vector of splitting times and sampling fraction.

Args:
    ages (list): vector of node ages
    sampling (float): sampling fraction (0, 1]
    min_bound (float): minimum birth rate

Returns:
    birth (float): optimized birth rate.
    death (float): optimized death rate.

functions.name: tact.lib.optim_yule
documentation:
Optimizes birth parameter under a Yule model, given a vector of splitting times and sampling fraction.

Args:
    ages (list): vector of node ages
    sampling (float): sampling fraction (0, 1]
    min_bound (float): minimum birth rate

Returns:
    birth (float): optimized birth rate.
    death (float): optimized death rate. Always 0.

functions.name: tact.lib.p1_exact
documentation: Exact version of `p1` using Decimal math.

functions.name: tact.lib.p1_orig
documentation: Original version of `p1`, here for testing and comparison purposes.

functions.name: tact.lib.p1
documentation:
Optimized version of `p1_orig` using common subexpression elimination and strength reduction
from exponentiation to multiplication.

functions.name: tact.lib.intp1_exact
documentation: Exact version of `intp1` using Decimal math.

functions.name: tact.lib.lik_constant
documentation:
Calculates the likelihood of a constant-rate birth-death process, conditioned
on the waiting times of a phylogenetic tree and degree of incomplete sampling.

Based off of the R function `TreePar::LikConstant` written by Tanja Stadler.

T. Stadler. On incomplete sampling under birth-death models and connections
to the sampling-based coalescent. Jour. Theo. Biol. 261: 58-66, 2009.

Args:
    vec (float, float): two element tuple of birth and death
    rho (float): sampling fraction
    t (list): vector of waiting times
    root (bool): include the root or not? (default: 1)
    survival (bool): assume survival of the process? (default: 1)

Returns:
    (float): likelihood of the birth-death process.

functions.name: tact.lib.crown_capture_probability
documentation:
Calculate the probability that a sample of `k` taxa from a clade
of `n` total taxa includes a root node, under a Yule process.

This equation is taken from:

Sanderson, M. J. 1996. How many taxa must be sampled to identify
the root node of a large clade? Systematic Biology 45:168-173

Args:
    n (int): total number of taxa
    k (int): sampled taxa

Returns:
    (float): probability of including a root node.

functions.name: tact.lib.get_new_times
documentation:
Simulates new speciation events in an incomplete phylogeny assuming a
constant-rate birth-death process.

Adapted from the R function `TreeSim::corsim` written by Tanja Stadler.

N. Cusimano, T. Stadler, S. Renner. A new method for handling missing
species in diversification analysis applicable to randomly or
non-randomly sampled phylogenies. Syst. Biol., 61(5): 785-792, 2012.

Args:
    ages (list): vector of waiting times
    birth (float): birth rate
    death (float): death rate
    missing (int): number of missing taxa to simulate
    told (float): maximum simulated age (default: `max(ages)`)
    tyoung (float): minimum simulated age bound (default: `0`)

Returns:
    (list): vector of simulated waiting times.
TODO: This could probably be optimized

modules.name: tact.cli_add_toml
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Try to assign tips to a pre-existing tree based on a TOML configuration file
Jonathan Chang, Aug 14, 2021

functions.name: tact.cli_add_toml.main
documentation: Add tips onto a BACKBONE phylogeny using a CONFIG file

classes.name: tact.cli_add_toml.TactConstraint
documentation: Class for keeping track of a constraint in TACT (positive or negative)

classes.name: tact.cli_add_toml.TactItem
documentation: Class for keeping track of things to TACT.

modules.name: tact.cli_add_taxa
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Try to assign tips to a pre-existing tree based on a taxonomy
Jonathan Chang, May 13, 2016

functions.name: tact.cli_add_taxa.get_new_branching_times
documentation: Get `n_total` new branching times for a `node`.

functions.name: tact.cli_add_taxa.main
documentation: Add tips onto a BACKBONE phylogeny using a TAXONOMY phylogeny.
